title: Non-record: Cross-layer parameter sharing + 4-bit QAT (RecurrentGPT)
author: evnkm

data.description: Preludes, a shared recurrent block iterated multiple times, and coda blocks replace a stack of unique transformer layers to share parameters across depth.
Uses aggressive grouped-query attention with fewer KV heads than attention heads.
Non-standard initialization mentioned as part of the preserved SOTA techniques.
FP16 tied embedding is preserved from the baseline.
Per-iteration learned gates allow the shared recurrent block to behave differently at each effective depth.
Phase-transition residual mixing applied across effective depth, including recurrent iterations.

novel_contributions: Cross-layer parameter sharing via a prelude → recurrent → coda architecture
Per-iteration learned gating for depth-dependent behavior in a shared recurrent block
4-bit quantization-aware training with straight-through estimator fake quantization
Unified quantization pipeline supporting int8 and 4-bit modes
Phase-transition residual mixing across effective depth
Preservation of baseline techniques such as FP16 tied embedding, Muon WD, overtone init, and sliding window evaluation
